title: Fix offset method signature mismatch with stats::offset
description: Error loading package: 'in method for offset with signature object=LatentNeuroVec: formal arguments (object = LatentNeuroVec) omitted in the method definition cannot be in the signature'. The stats::offset generic has different signature than our method definition.
close_reason: Fixed offset method signature to use 'object' parameter matching stats::offset. Fixed corrupted validation function. All 1498 tests pass.

title: Fix HierarchicalBasisTemplate class/doc mismatch
description:
S4 class codoc mismatch for HierarchicalBasisTemplate:
Code slots: atoms, gram_factor, levels, loadings, mask, meta, parents, space
Docs slots: gram_factor, levels, loadings, mask, parents, space

Missing documentation for 'atoms' slot. Add @slot atoms documentation.
notes: Note: The Rd file (man/HierarchicalBasisTemplate-class.Rd) actually DOES contain @slot documentation for 'atoms' and 'meta'. The codoc mismatch is likely caused by Rd parsing errors (fmrilatent-3rr) - the unknown macro '\item' errors prevent R CMD check from properly parsing the slots section. Fix fmrilatent-3rr first; this issue may auto-resolve.
close_reason: HierarchicalBasisTemplate slots documented and codoc passes

title: Encapsulate .fmrilatent_registry and add lifecycle API
description: Code uses a global mutable env registry (.fmrilatent_registry in R/latent_handles.R) to cache materialized BasisHandle/LoadingsHandle matrices. This has no lifecycle (clear/reset), makes tests order-dependent, and makes memory usage hard to manage.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add exported functions to inspect/clear registry (e.g., fmrilatent_registry_clear(type=c('basis','loadings','all')))
- Add internal helpers so handle materialization uses the same registry API
- Add tests proving cache hit/miss behavior and that clear() forces re-materialization
- Document expected semantics (session-local cache)
close_reason: Registry lifecycle API implemented with fmrilatent_registry_clear(), fmrilatent_registry_list(), fmrilatent_registry_stats(). Test file test-registry.R has 28 passing tests covering cache hit/miss behavior and re-materialization after clear.

title: Fix Rd file parsing errors in HierarchicalBasisTemplate and build_hierarchical_template
description:
R CMD check reports Rd parsing errors in two files:

**HierarchicalBasisTemplate-class.Rd:**
- Line 28, 30: unknown macro '\item'
- Line 33: unexpected END_OF_INPUT
- Lines 28, 30: Lost braces

**build_hierarchical_template.Rd:**
- Line 30, 32: unknown macro '\item'  
- Line 34: unexpected section header '\value'
- Line 37: unexpected section header '\description'
- Lines 30, 32, 34-36, 37-41: Lost braces

These are structural Rd syntax errors (not missing content). May need to:
1. Regenerate Rd files with devtools::document()
2. Check for invisible characters or encoding issues
3. Verify roxygen2 comments are properly formatted

Note: The source R files (R/all_class.R, R/hierarchical_template.R) appear correct, so this may be a regeneration/caching issue.
close_reason: Rd files are correctly formatted - may have been regenerated since issue was filed

- Include at least one expect_warning() or expect_message() test where behavior is intentionally noisy
- Tests run via Rscript -e "devtools::test()"

title: Harden Gram solves in encode_spec to avoid singular solve() crashes
description: encode_spec.spec_space_hrbf() and encode_spec.spec_st() compute Gram matrices and call solve(gram) directly. This can fail for ill-conditioned/singular Gram matrices (e.g., redundant atoms, tiny masks, unlucky params).
acceptance_criteria:
- Introduce a shared helper for Gram inversion/solve (ridge + Matrix::solve fallback)
- Update encode_spec.spec_space_hrbf and encode_spec.spec_st to use it
- Add tests that previously-singular cases do not error and return finite coefficients
- Keep results identical (within tolerance) for well-conditioned cases
close_reason: Added .robust_gram_solve() helper with ridge regularization and SVD fallback. Updated encode_spec.spec_space_hrbf and encode_spec.spec_st to use it.

title: Fix build_hierarchical_template() diag() error with small matrices
description:
build_hierarchical_template() fails with small matrices due to a diag() error.

**Location**: R/hierarchical_template.R

**Error**: When called with small matrices (e.g., 2x2x2 mask), the function fails with an error related to diag().

**Impact**: 5 tests skipped in test-hierarchical_template.R:
- Line 101: build_hierarchical_template creates valid template with single level
- Line 121: build_hierarchical_template creates valid template with two levels
- Line 159: encode_hierarchical returns LatentNeuroVec with correct dimensions
- Line 196: project_hierarchical returns coefficient matrix
- Line 244: save and load hierarchical template round-trip

**To Fix**: Debug build_hierarchical_template() to handle small matrices correctly. The diag() function may be receiving unexpected input (vector vs matrix, or Matrix object vs base matrix).

**Test**: Remove skip() calls from the 5 tests in test-hierarchical_template.R once fixed.
close_reason: Fixed by handling small parcels (n_loc < 3) with base R eigen() instead of RSpectra::eigs which requires dimension >= 3. Updated both hierarchical_template.R and slepian_spatial.R. All hierarchical template tests now pass.

Affected functions:
- benchmark_roundtrip: rnorm, predict

- plot_spatial_atom: aggregate
close_reason: Fixed: added importFrom for graphics/stats functions, globalVariables() for ggplot2 NSE, and @description to build_hierarchical_template

title: Fix load_all/document failure: missing S4 generic 'map'
description:
Observed during roxygen2/devtools: devtools::document() -> load_all() fails in R/latent_neurovector.R with setMethod(): no existing definition for function 'map'.

Likely root cause: methods::isGeneric() uses a cached lookup when called with missing(where). That cache can return TRUE even if the generic function object is not present in the target environment (repro: setGeneric('map', ...); rm(map); isGeneric('map') == TRUE but isGeneric('map', where=.GlobalEnv) == FALSE).

Fix: Avoid the cache by calling isGeneric(..., where = topenv(parent.frame())) (explicit where) before any setMethod('map', ...) and for any other critical generics. Ensure map generic is created in the package namespace before methods are registered.

title: slepian_temporal_handle auto-id missing backend causes cache collision
description: Auto-generated id for slepian_temporal_handle uses sprintf("slepian-t-%d-%.4f-%d", n_time, bandwidth, k) but does not include backend parameter. Handles with same n_time/bandwidth/k but different backends (tridiag vs dense) will share the same cache entry, potentially returning wrong results. Fix: include backend in id format: sprintf("slepian-t-%d-%.4f-%d-%s", n_time, bandwidth, k, backend)
close_reason: Fixed: added backend to auto-id format in sprintf

title: Fix Matrix::sparseQR missing dependency
description:
Missing or unexported object: 'Matrix::sparseQR'

The code references Matrix::sparseQR but this is not exported from Matrix package. Need to either:
1. Use an alternative approach
2. Access via Matrix:::sparseQR (not recommended)
3. Check if this was removed in newer Matrix versions
close_reason: Removed Matrix::sparseQR usage; use base qr()
